How can Digital Twins improve real-time monitoring and predictive maintenance in construction?
==============================================================================================

Ellen Flannery|17 July, 2024

**Kevin Soohoo, Senior Director of the AEC Practice at Egnyte**

![Image 4](https://www.intelligentbuild.tech/wp-content/uploads/sites/63/2024/07/KEVIN-SOOHOO.png)

Kevin Soohoo, Senior Director of the AEC Practice at Egnyte

Digital Twins enable construction companies to model and analyse physical properties in perpetuity. The benefits of a Digital Twin are quickly transitioning from a nice to have to a necessity for companies doing work that demands efficiency, accuracy and compliance with evolving regulatory standards. Implementing Digital Twins in projects enhances project control by helping companies achieve sustainability goals, adhere to regulatory guidelines and more.

Integrating Digital Twins with the Building Information Modelling (BIM) process empowers firms to address key industry challenges such as reduced productivity, profitability and performance.

During construction, real-time monitoring systems can track progress, identify potential issues and analyse the performance of various systems for quick decision-making and problem resolution. Additionally, Digital Twins allow for the simulation of different construction scenarios and the analysis of potential outcomes. This predictive analytics capability helps anticipate issues, optimise construction processes and improve overall project efficiency.

Digital Twins foster better collaboration among stakeholders by providing a shared, up-to-date view of the project. This enhances communication and coordination across different teams and disciplines. Ensuring that files are the most current versions and reducing duplication makes it easier to locate information and avoid data sprawl. Effective version control and data management will become increasingly important as regulators mandate comprehensive safety regulations requiring extensive record retention.

Beyond the construction phase, Digital Twins provide valuable data for the maintenance and operation of buildings. This supports proactive maintenance, reduces downtime and extends the building’s lifespan. Digital Twins also benefit companies that must comply with safety regulations like the Building Safety Act by retaining accurate and detailed records of their projects long after completion. A realistic project rendering enables firms responsible for monitoring construction, maintenance, and ongoing safety compliance to perform these tasks efficiently and effectively.

**Jeremy Suard, CEO and Co-founder at Exodigo**

![Image 5](https://www.intelligentbuild.tech/wp-content/uploads/sites/63/2024/07/JEREMY-SUARD-1024x1024.jpg)

Jeremy Suard, CEO and Co-founder at Exodigo

Digital Twins play a crucial role in construction safety and efficient use of project resources. A key element that is often overlooked in terms of ongoing monitoring and predictive maintenance is the need to understand what lies beneath the surface.

The unknown of the underground creates massive challenges for construction teams – from potentially dangerous utility strikes early in the build, to costly, unplanned rerouting work along the way or unstable foundations in the future. A powerful combination of advanced sensors, 3D imaging and AI technologies can now create detailed Digital Twins of the underground landscape that can scale to meet changing needs so project leaders can see the full picture, including what lies beneath the surface and properly plan ahead.

Underground Digital Twin technology is revolutionising the construction industry by providing a precise view of the underground – utility lines, bedrock and other potential challenges or changes. Armed with this critical data, construction managers can make informed decisions about what and where to build, while reducing risks, limiting redesigns and minimising delays due to uncharted obstacles.

A leader in underground Digital Twin technology, Exodigo’s multi-sensing underground mapping platform empowers construction managers to plan infrastructure projects that are delivered on time and within budget – fostering sustainable growth and development.

By building smarter, we can avoid problems instead of reacting to them. With a digital record, underground information is perpetually available to any organisation stakeholder. Digital Twins simplify the often challenging task of institutional knowledge transfer in our industry.

**Michael Evans, Senior Director, Product Structures Division, Trimble**

![Image 6](https://www.intelligentbuild.tech/wp-content/uploads/sites/63/2024/07/Michael-evans-trimble-FINAL.jpg)

Michael Evans, Senior Director, Product Structures Division, Trimble

Digital Twins are the way to connect the physical and digital worlds. BIM models of assets comprise federated models from different trades: architecture, engineering, fabrication, construction, MEP and more can be further enriched with data from 3D scans and connected devices (IoT) and constructible, real-time data to turn as-built models into Digital Twins.

Elevating 3D models to Digital Twins increases analytical capabilities throughout all phases of a construction project. Digital Twins offer a better way — a visual common ground that offers invaluable cohesion through which construction phases can communicate more effectively. Component dimensions, model details, working conditions and more can be embedded into content-enabled models. Applying IoT devices delivering up-to-date information to their digital equivalent, the 3D model is then enabled to directly represent that physical asset on the spot. This helps monitor asset status.

All too often, important details get lost in translation as a model progresses down the chain of command. Digital Twin technology offers accurate, real-time updates of building progress that will reveal any deviations from 3D models. Trimble solutions make on-the-spot data collection and design verification faster and less resource-intensive.

Having the most up-to-date information on products or fabricated pieces of a structure (like the source of the material, the certification of the welds, etc.) embedded into content-enabled models allows for ‘Digital Twin builds’ — the quickest, most efficient and most accurate fabrication possible. Trimble can also collect this level of constructed/fabricated/manufactured data which would make the Digital Twin richer when Trimble products are used.

This approach reduces errors and rework, along with the time and money they waste. And, on-site scheduling is enhanced through real just-in-time production and delivery of fabricated components.

When the project is complete, Digital Twins can be handed over to the client to help support ongoing structural enhancements. For example, if a client is interested in the environmental impact of their structure, a Digital Twin can track energy demand, CO2 emissions and indoor air quality to help shape future green initiatives. A Digital Twin can be a value-added asset for clients looking to leverage data in future optimisation projects.

As for existing buildings or infrastructure, Digital Twins can be applied as a starting point for proactive maintenance. This starts with the creation of high-resolution 3D scans through by using mobile mapping systems or 3D (laser) scanners and applying AI-enabled technology to identify structural issues. The next step involves deploying the software to design sustainable mitigative measures based on construction technology that supports high-accuracy location-based construction. With that, the possibility unfolds to create a live Digital Twin that can serve as the basis for future maintenance, with facilities and maintenance data added to the twin on an ongoing basis, to support the asset during its entire lifecycle.

Digital Twins are, first and foremost, analytical tools that offer unprecedented insight into the condition of field investments and help inform future operations.

**Hamid Alemohammad, Chief Operating Officer at Brickeye**

![Image 7](https://www.intelligentbuild.tech/wp-content/uploads/sites/63/2024/07/Hamid_1__2_-edited.png)

Hamid Alemohammad, Chief Operating Officer at Brickeye

Real-time monitoring and predictive maintenance are critical considerations during the lifecycle of construction projects, from pre- to post-construction. The implementation of a Digital Twin can help construction stakeholders better understand and protect their project assets to make insightful and informed predictions on future performance trends, increasing efficiency, meeting compliance requirements and enhancing site productivity based on historical, current and predictive data.

As construction proceeds through the project lifecycle, different types of data are continuously captured – via on-site sensors and monitoring devices, combined with metadata obtained from other sources – allowing for a digital replica to be updated in real-time. Throughout construction, stakeholders can use this Digital Twin platform to gain a multi-dimensional view of the performance of the construction site – predicting trends, detecting risks, setting up automation for risk controls and optimising project schedules.

With the addition of real-time monitoring, Digital Twins mitigates construction risk through early detection of discrepancies – keeping project stakeholders updated on any potential abnormal conditions, incidents or tasks. Before the advent of digital technologies, frequent physical site observations and inspections were required to verify work done and determine the project status. With a Digital Twin platform and other on-site technologies, such as sensors and cameras, augmented data can verify the progress in real-time, without needing to step foot on the job site.

Other benefits include:

*   **Improved worker safety:** Digital Twins enable companies to identify risk-associated areas to prevent unsafe activities and incidents in hazardous zones, and to ensure safe and effective usage of materials.

*   **Enhanced sustainability and efficiency:** Real-time data is collected and analysed to generate insights into equipment utilisation, material tracking and energy consumption.

*   **Greener construction:** Digital Twins can examine and predict the carbon footprint of different aspects of construction projects, offering insights on an ongoing basis about potential process improvements to reduce emissions.

Over the past several years, Digital Twin technology has made significant strides to modernise the construction industry and improve project construction with real-time monitoring and predictive analytics. Unlocking an advanced level of automation allows stakeholders to optimise processes, improve operational efficiency and mitigate risks. With comprehensive data and analytics, Digital Twins can aid in reducing waste, delays and reworks – while prioritising worker safety and sustainability in the wake of climate change – giving stakeholders the information and tools they need to make better-informed decisions to protect their projects.

## 

Imagine having a real-time, updated mirror image of your company’s activities that provides instant information on performance, opportunities, and risks. That’s precisely what real-time [digital twin](https://www.toobler.com/blog/what-is-digital-twin) integration brings to the future.

But here’s a question: What’s the point of a digital twin if its data is outdated the moment you access it? Without real-time integration, it’s like using an old blueprint—practical but not accurate.

Surprising Fact: An [analysis](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S277266222300005X) projects that the growing need for real-time data analytics and predictive insights will propel the digital twin market to $73.5 billion by 2027.

The real-time integration in [digital twin technology](https://www.toobler.com/digital-twin-solutions) guarantees that companies can make data-driven decisions instantaneously, from supply chain logistics optimization to machine failure prediction.

So, how are industries being reshaped by this real-time connectedness, and why is it now a requirement rather than an idealistic idea?

As a first step, let us understand what real-time integration is.

## What is Real-Time Data Integration?

At its core, real-time data integration refers to instantaneously gathering, consolidating, and making data available from various sources. Instead of waiting for periodic updates, we're talking about a dynamic flow of information updated as events occur.

Imagine watching a live broadcast of a sports game versus catching the recorded highlights later. Real-time data integration is like having a front-row seat to the live game, experiencing every twist and turn as it unfolds.

Now, you might wonder, "How is this different from the other data integration I've heard about?" Great question!

That's often batch data integration you're thinking of. Imagine a bakery that makes batches of cookies. They mix the ingredients, bake a batch, and then move on to the next one. Similarly, in batch data integration, data is collected in chunks or batches at scheduled intervals—say, every hour or once a day—and then processed.

To contrast:

**Real-time data integration** is like a constantly flowing river. It is continuously updating and offering fresh insights from moment to moment.

**Batch data integration**, on the other hand, is like a series of lakes where water (or data, in our case) is periodically released or updated.

While batch data integration has its merits and is cost-effective in certain scenarios, the immediate insights from real-time integration can be a game-changer. This is important, especially in industries where staying updated can make all the difference between seizing an opportunity and missing it entirely.

So, to ensure your digital twin is as accurate as possible, understanding the nuances of real-time data integration is crucial.

## Why Real-Time Data Integration is Crucial for Digital Twins?

So, what precisely is a [**digital twin**](https://www.toobler.com/blog/digital-twin)? A digital twin is a virtual representation of a real system or thing. To ensure that it appropriately represents the current situation of its real-world equivalent, this [digital twin model](https://www.toobler.com/blog/digital-twin-model) is updated regularly using real-time data.

Sensors and data points that provide real-time data to the digital model are built into digital twins. Any changes in the physical environment are immediately reflected in the digital twin because of this real-time compatibility.

For instance, the [**digital twin in manufacturing**](https://www.toobler.com/blog/digital-twins-in-manufacturing) will instantly display machine wear, enabling prompt maintenance before a failure occurs.

Let's discuss the importance of having real-time data integration for digital twins.

### 1. Enhancing the Accuracy and Reliability of Digital Twins

Imagine crafting a meticulous model of a city right down to each traffic light. Now, if that model only updates once a day, a roadblock or a sudden parade might throw everything off. The same goes for digital twins.

Without real-time data, a digital twin is just a static model - accurate at one point but potentially obsolete the next. Real-time data helps here. They ensure that digital twins are not just reflections but dynamically evolving entities that mirror their physical counterparts with impeccable precision.

### 2. Real-time Decision-making and Instant Feedback

Think of piloting a drone. Would you prefer a live feed or periodic image updates?

With real-time data integration, digital twins provide an immediate overview of the entire business operation. This means you can spot trends, anomalies, or potential roadblocks instantly and react accordingly.

No more "I wish I had known this earlier!" moments. It's about acting swiftly and smartly, guided by real-time insights.

### **3. Bridging the Physical-Digital Gap Effectively**

This is where the excitement really starts to build. Digital twin integration with real-time data helps to close the gap between the physical and digital worlds, which used to stay apart.

Businesses are able to establish a quick and smooth connection between the digital and physical worlds as sensors and devices continuously transmit data into real-time digital twins. It gives you an unmatched advantage in efficiency and decision-making.

Consider your digital twin as a dynamic, real-time copy of your company's operations. Digital twin integration serves as a lifeline, guaranteeing that it stays precise, responsive, and useful.

In the absence of this real-time connectivity, the digital twin runs the risk of being reduced to a static reflection. Though its full potential in real-time decision-making, process improvement, and predictive analytics will not be realized, it will still be helpful.

### 4. Less Slowdowns and Predictive Maintenance

One of its key benefits is real-time integration, which makes predictive maintenance easier.

Digital twins constantly track the state of the machinery to anticipate possible equipment breakdowns and plan upkeep before problems arise.

This proactive strategy lowers expenses, increases asset longevity, and minimizes downtime.

In the aviation sector, for instance, real-time data from aircraft components can forecast maintenance requirements, avert unanticipated malfunctions, and guarantee flight safety.

### 5. Improved Cooperation and Communication

Additionally, real-time integration improves teamwork and communication. A single, continuously updated source of truth allows all parties involved to have the same information. This improves coordination and enabling well-informed decision-making.

Consider [digital twins in construction](https://www.toobler.com/blog/digital-twin-in-construction).

For example, all team members can receive real-time information on project progress, which guarantees everyone is aware of the situation and lowers the possibility of mistakes and delays.

So, it is imperative that we harness the full potential of real-time data in our digital twins. It is not just smart—it's absolutely essential.

## Key Components and Mechanisms in Real-Time Data Integration for Digital Twins

Real-time data integration, a critical foundation for robust digital twins, is underpinned by several key components and mechanisms.

Let's explore each of them.

![Digital Twin System](https://a.storyblok.com/f/122804/3334x2078/3721cf337e/digital-twin-system.webp)

![Digital Twin System](https://a.storyblok.com/f/122804/3334x2078/3721cf337e/digital-twin-system.webp)

### Data Sources

#### 1. IoT Devices

A cornerstone for real-time data capture is IoT devices. This could range from smart thermostats to advanced manufacturing robots. They actively monitor and transmit data about their operations and environments.

Also, take a look at [how IoT helps Digital twins](https://www.toobler.com/blog/digital-twin-iot).

#### 2. Sensors

The sensors give us real-time information about temperature, pressure, humidity, and more. They're the ears and eyes on the ground, capturing the environment's intricate details.

#### 3. External Systems

These are essentially third-party applications like ERP systems or CRMs. They provide critical data, from sales figures to supply chain metrics, that integrate into a digital twin environment.

### Middleware

The middleware platforms and tools act as the conductor. They ensure seamless communication and synchronization among all the instruments. They're the bridge, facilitating data transfer from the myriad sources to the digital twin.

Following are some middleware you need to be aware of -

#### 1. Integration Platforms

These are designed to seamlessly connect disparate data sources. This ensures a consistent and streamlined flow of data into the digital twin environment.

#### 2. Message Brokers

These tools manage and streamline the communication between data sources and the digital twin. They often use protocols like MQTT or AMQP to ensure data is transferred efficiently and reliably.

#### 3. API Management

With numerous devices and platforms involved, APIs facilitate the real-time exchange of data between them. And their management ensures efficiency and security.

### Data Processing

#### 1. Stream Processing

As data is continuously generated, stream processing tools handle and analyze this data on the fly without the need for storage.

#### 2. Real-time Analytics

This provides immediate insights into the incoming data. As a result, it enables businesses to detect anomalies, trends, or critical changes instantaneously.

#### 3. Filtering

Given the vast amounts of data generated, it's crucial to filter out the noise. This ensures that only relevant data is integrated into the digital twin.

### Storage and Retrieval

#### 1. Cloud Storage

Think of this as the vast library where data are archived. Cloud storage offers a scalable and accessible repository for all the integrated data.

#### 2. Edge Computing

For scenarios where latency is a concern, processing data closer to its source (i.e., on the edge) ensures real-time integration and minimizes delays.

#### 3. In-memory Databases

As the name suggests, data is stored in the system's main memory (rather than on disk) for faster retrieval. This allows digital twins to access and act upon data with lightning speed.

The robustness of a digital twin largely depends on its real-time data integration infrastructure. You can build digital twin solutions for unparalleled insights and decision-making prowess by recognizing and effectively leveraging these components and mechanisms.

## Challenges in Implementing Real-Time Data Integration

Yes, real-time integration is necessary for the efficient functioning of digital twins. But [digital twin implementation](https://www.toobler.com/blog/digital-twin-implementation) comes with challenges.

Understanding these [challenges in digital twin implementation](https://www.toobler.com/blog/challenges-in-digital-twin-implementation) is the first step to addressing them effectively:

### Latency Issues and Ensuring Data is Truly "Real-time"

Transmission Delays:

Even with the best infrastructure, the time it takes for data to travel from its source to the processing unit can introduce delays.

Processing Speed:

Once data reaches its destination, the speed at which it's processed can also introduce latency. This is the case, especially if the volume is high or the computations are complex.

Network Congestion:

High traffic or limited bandwidth can cause data packet delays or losses, affecting the "real-time" aspect.

### Handling Vast Data Volumes from Multiple Sources

Scalability Concerns:

As the number of connected devices and sensors grows, the infrastructure must scale proportionally to handle increased data flows.

Resource Constraints:

Physical limitations, like memory or processing power, can be quickly overwhelmed by vast data streams.

Storage Dilemmas:

While not all real-time data needs to be stored, determining what to retain and where to store it becomes a challenge. Again, this is a big challenge, especially when dealing with vast volumes.

### Ensuring Data Accuracy and Integrity

![ensuring data accuracy and integrity is challenging](https://a.storyblok.com/f/122804/3334x2078/58f9f06344/ensuring-data-accuracy-and-integrity-is-challenging.webp)

![ensuring data accuracy and integrity is challenging](https://a.storyblok.com/f/122804/3334x2078/58f9f06344/ensuring-data-accuracy-and-integrity-is-challenging.webp)

Data Veracity:

Not all data coming in real-time is of high quality. Erroneous readings from a malfunctioning sensor, for instance, can skew analytics.

Data Consistency:

With multiple sources, there's a risk of receiving conflicting or redundant data, complicating processing and analysis.

Security Threats:

Real-time data streams can be vulnerable to breaches or tampering, potentially compromising data integrity. The [digital twin security](https://www.toobler.com/blog/digital-twin-security) practices must be adopted to remove this challenge.

### Integration Complexities with Existing Systems

Legacy Systems:

Older systems might not be designed to support real-time data flows. This could lead to costly and complex upgrades or replacements.

Interoperability:

Different systems come with different data formats and standards. Integrating them smoothly in real time can be challenging.

Change Management:

Shifting organizational processes to accommodate real-time data integration can face resistance. This is the case if it disrupts established workflows.

While these challenges might seem daunting, they're part and parcel of pioneering change. With strategic planning, technological investment, and a commitment to continuous learning and adaptation, organizations can navigate these hurdles, reaping the immense benefits real-time data integration offers.

With that being said, let's take a look at some of the best practices to follow.

## Best Practices for Effective Real-Time Data Integration

Here are some best practices that can guide you toward seamless integration.

### Prioritizing Data Sources and Determining the Relevance of Real-Time Data

#### 1. Assess Data Relevance

Not all data warrants real-time integration. Determine which data points are mission-critical for immediate analysis and which can be processed in batches or at regular intervals.

#### 2. Segment Sources

Categorize data sources based on their priority. High-priority sources might need dedicated channels or enhanced bandwidth to ensure uninterrupted data flow.

#### 3. Streamline Data Flows

Reduce data redundancy by merging or aggregating similar data streams, leading to more efficient processing.

[![](https://a.storyblok.com/f/122804/1600x500/d994cd9171/cta-digital-twin.webp)](https://www.toobler.com/contact)Utilizing Standardized Data Formats and Protocols

![](https://a.storyblok.com/f/122804/1600x500/d994cd9171/cta-digital-twin.webp)

#### 1. Adopt Universal Protocols

Embrace widely accepted protocols like MQTT or AMQP designed for real-time communication.

#### 2. Data Normalization

Ensure that data, regardless of its source, is transformed into a standardized format before integration, simplifying analytics and interpretation.

#### 3. Regularly Update Standards

The world of data is dynamic. Review and update data standards periodically to remain compatible with emerging technologies and practices.

### Ensuring Robust Security and Encryption Methods

![Measures to ensure data security](https://a.storyblok.com/f/122804/3334x2078/fbd6de9dfd/measures-to-ensure-data-security.webp)

![Measures to ensure data security](https://a.storyblok.com/f/122804/3334x2078/fbd6de9dfd/measures-to-ensure-data-security.webp)

#### 1. End-to-end Encryption

Encrypt data in transit and at rest to safeguard against breaches and unauthorized access.

#### 2. Authentication Protocols

Implement rigorous authentication methods for data sources to prevent data tampering or injection of malicious data.

#### 3. Continuous Monitoring

Employ real-time monitoring tools to detect anomalies or potential security threats instantly, allowing for swift counteraction.

### Leveraging Edge Computing for Decentralized Data Processing

#### 1. Localize Data Processing

By processing data closer to its source, edge computing reduces latency, ensuring that insights derived are as current as possible.

#### 2. Reduce Bandwidth Strain

Decentralized processing means less data is transmitted over the network, preserving bandwidth for other crucial operations.

#### 3. Enhanced Reliability

With edge computing, even if a central system faces downtime, localized processing can continue unhindered, ensuring that real-time insights remain uninterrupted.

By adhering to these best practices, organizations can set themselves up for success.

### **1. Real-time monitoring and visibility**

Digital twins provide a 360-degree view of the data centre, integrating data from servers, cooling systems, power distribution, and environmental factors. This ability enables operators to detect anomalies and optimize operations proactively: simulating different operational scenarios to identify the most efficient one; adjusting settings automatically based on real-time data to optimize energy utilization and resource allocation; and improving productivity through reducing bottlenecks and removing inefficiencies.

### **2. Predictive maintenance and reduced downtime**

Machine learning models predict failures before they occur, reducing unplanned downtime. Equipment lifespan is extended by optimizing maintenance schedules based on actual conditions rather than fixed intervals.

### **3. Energy efficiency and sustainability**

A digital twin optimizes power and cooling systems, reducing energy waste. It helps meet green energy goals by simulating energy-saving strategies before implementation and it supports carbon footprint reduction by balancing workloads efficiently.

### **4. Capacity planning and resource optimization**

IT Advisor simulates future workloads, enabling better planning for IT expansion or consolidation; avoids over-provisioning, reducing unnecessary capital and operational costs; and balances workloads dynamically, ensuring optimal utilization of resources.

### **5. Disaster recovery and risk management**

A digital twin simulates failure scenarios that can happen, such as power outages, cooling failures, and cyberattacks, to test and refine recovery strategies. It enhances resilience by proactively identifying vulnerabilities in the infrastructure.

### **6. Automation and AI-driven optimization**

AI-driven automation helps in workload balancing, cooling efficiency, and proactive incident response. It reduces manual interventions, leading to lower operational costs and human error.

### **7. Regulatory compliance and security**

Our solution ensures compliance with industry standards (e.g., Uptime Institute, ISO 27001, GDPR) by continuously monitoring and logging critical parameters and it enhances [cybersecurity](https://blog.se.com/datacenter/dcim/2024/05/29/when-threats-are-headed-for-your-it-infrastructure-stay-calm-and-manage-on-with-dcim-and-lessons-from-jaws/) by modelling potential attack vectors and their impacts.

**Save money and maximize ROI with a digital twin**
---------------------------------------------------

These seven factors all add up to cost savings and the ability to maximize ROI. [IT Advisor](https://www.se.com/us/en/product-range/66103-ecostruxure-it-advisor/#overview) is a solution that helps our customers reduce energy and maintenance costs through predictive insights. It avoids expensive downtime and equipment failures and enables scenario modelling to evaluate investment decisions before execution.

# Connect Real-Time IoT Data to Digital Twins for 3D Remote Monitoring

![](https://developer-blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/ov-social-ignite-iot-tech-blog-1600x900-1-1024x576.png)

As enterprises increasingly integrate AI into their industrial operations to deliver more automated and autonomous facilities, more operations teams are becoming centralized in remote operations centers. From these centers, these teams monitor, operate, and provide expert guidance to distributed production sites.

A new generation of 3D remote monitoring solutions, powered by advancements in computer graphics, accelerated computing, and open standards like [Universal Scene Description (OpenUSD)](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/omniverse/usd/?srsltid=AfmBOopS1Zsyy7OCkv7KiWNhYzJxpeIbnu3MpbtGarsXe8SmOo7okA7w), are emerging to transform industrial operations by removing silos between IT and operational technology. This enables operations experts to quickly identify and solve problems, simulate operations at scale, and optimize production.

To help developers build advanced, AI-enabled 3D applications and workflows to support operations teams, NVIDIA and Microsoft Azure have developed a reference architecture, Microsoft [Azure Arc Jumpstart](https://www.arcjumpstart.com/nvidia_omniverse_azure_operations_twin) guide, and a public [GitHub repo](https://github.com/microsoft/NVIDIA-Omniverse-Azure-Operations-Twin). These resources guide developers step by step in how to connect physically accurate 3D models of industrial systems and production environments to real-time data from Azure IoT Operations and Power BI reports.

## From 2D to 3D remote monitoring of industrial operations

Traditionally, operations teams have monitored industrial systems and facilities through 2D dashboards and data visualizations, which can make it challenging to fully contextualize operational data and the complex relationships between factory equipment, processes, and workflows.

With the advancements in computer graphics and generative AI, operations teams are embracing more interactive and intuitive tools, including physically accurate [3D digital twins](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/glossary/digital-twin/) and simulation environments that provide deeper insights into systems and facilities, resulting in accelerated decisions and outcomes.

When these AI-enabled 3D solutions are connected to live data at the edge, they unlock many benefits for enterprises and remote monitoring teams, including:

## Building a new era of 3D remote monitoring solutions

Developers looking to deliver benefits like these to enterprises and their remote operations teams can reference the [Azure Arc Jumpstart](https://www.arcjumpstart.com/nvidia_omniverse_azure_operations_twin) guide and [microsoft/NVIDIA-Omniverse-Azure-Operations-Twin](https://github.com/microsoft/NVIDIA-Omniverse-Azure-Operations-Twin) GitHub repo to learn how to build 3D remote monitoring applications and workflows powered by Azure IoT Operations, Power BI, OpenUSD, and [NVIDIA Omniverse Kit App Streaming](https://docs.omniverse.nvidia.com/ovas/latest/index.html).

The [Azure Arc Jumpstart](https://www.arcjumpstart.com/nvidia_omniverse_azure_operations_twin) guides developers through a series of foundational steps to build an end-to-end workflow for 3D remote monitoring, including:

![A diagram showcasing the reference architecture and workflow within the  Azure Arc Jumpstart guide. This diagram shows the process; Factory, Azure Event Hub, ISV Web App, Omniverse Backend, and finally OpenUSD Assets.](https://developer-blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/reference-architecture-workflow-azure-jumpstart-guide-1.png)
![A diagram showcasing the reference architecture and workflow within the  Azure Arc Jumpstart guide. This diagram shows the process; Factory, Azure Event Hub, ISV Web App, Omniverse Backend, and finally OpenUSD Assets.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%201859%201044%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

The reference workflow and [Azure Arc Jumpstart](https://www.arcjumpstart.com/nvidia_omniverse_azure_operations_twin) guide demonstrate how dynamic IoT data from the factory floor can be used to update a Power BI report that is integrated with an OpenUSD-based application developed on the [NVIDIA Omniverse](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/omniverse/) platform.

Using the guide, developers learn how to connect IoT data to the Power Platform and how to build a web application embedding an Omniverse powered 3D viewport and Power BI visuals. This enables you to build an end user experience that provides real-time production data in the context of a physically accurate 3D visualization.

The guide details deployment of Kit-based applications via Omniverse Kit App Streaming on the Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), enabling horizontal scaling of NVIDIA RTX GPU-accelerated compute resources to remote operators.

You’re encouraged to customize the reference web application and the embedded 3D viewport for your desired use cases and workflows.

What is a Real-Time Digital Twin? | 2024

Digital twins have been revolutionizing manufacturing, systems analysis, customer service, medical industries, marketing, and so much more. The value of digital twins - especially manufacturing and processes-related digital twins - lies in their ability to accelerate decision-making, promote sustainability, avoid critical mishaps, increase productivity, and more.Â

Real-time digital twins, with their ability to adapt to new data constantly, are only adding to these benefits. Weâll explore what a real-time digital twin is and how organizations can benefit from real-time digital twin technology.

What is a Real-Time Digital Twin?

Digital twins are digital representations of real-world objects, people, or processes used to observe past and present data, simulate potential future outcomes, accelerate holistic understanding, and more.

Real-time digital twins receive data in a continuous stream, tracking dynamic information about a physical device to allow for optimized and constantly up-to-date analysis. These real-time digital twins help identify problems and speed up response times to such problems, and they allow for on-the-fly adjustments to reach optimal efficiency.Â

For our purposes, weâll focus on âpeopleâ-type digital twins, which are digital representations of human beings. When these digital twins have real-time capabilities, they can be created instantly and can interact with humans in real time.

An example of this would be Tavusâ talking-head digital twin. AI models at Tavus can generate your very own digital twin for instant video generation at scale.

Real-Time Digital Twin vs Traditional Digital Twin

Real-time and traditional digital twins are differentiated by how they make use of the data that creates and sustains them. Conventional digital twins collect data about an object and then store that data for later analysis, whether to find and address problems or to conduct simulations.

Real-time digital twins, on the other hand, utilize the data they receive immediately, allowing for in-the-moment use with up-to-date information. For example, you might generate your digital twin for use as a live chat avatar with real-time processing of customer preferences, creating a more engaging and personalized experience.

How Does Real-Time Digital Twin Technology Work?Â

Digital twin technology gathers data from physical sensors (or, in the case of video-based digital twins, from sample videos) and uses the data to replicate the object, person, or process in a digital environment. Typically, digital twins store data and then perform analytics, since traditional methods of analysis have difficulty meeting the computational demands of real-time digital twins.Â

Thatâs why real-time digital twins require scalable software architecture so they can analyze streaming data on the fly and provide faster responses. One method to address this need is the use of in-memory computing, which can deliver messages to digital twins within milliseconds.

Real-Time Digital Twin Examples & Use CasesÂ

A wide variety of industries already use digital twin technology. Weâll explore the importance of real-time integration for improving the effectiveness of digital twins in each of these applications.

Marketing & SalesÂ

Real-time digital twin technology for people-type digital twins learns the nuances of your facial expressions, movements, and voice rapidly. This allows your digital avatar to reflect you with a high level of precision. That speed enables personalized video generation at scale.

Another important feature you want from your digital twin generator is the ability to use your digital twin to create thousands of marketing and sales videos immediately while retaining that highly-realistic replica of your image and voice.Â

Thatâs why Tavus is an excellent choice for your real-time digital twin. With scaled personalization options and unlimited video generation, you can create engaging sales videos to improve your conversion rates.Â

Generate personalized marketing videos with Tavusâs real-time digital twin capabilities!

ConstructionÂ

Digital twins in the construction industry help streamline the planning, budget, design, and actual construction phases. Construction digital twins are virtual representations of buildings or infrastructure projects, using data from sensors, BIM models, and more to optimize performance and improve decision-making.Â

If you make that digital twin real-time, with constant streams of data from the construction site, you can identify problems as they come up, rather than having to fix issues later and extend construction time.Â

And once a building is constructed, its digital twin can monitor the entire structure, enabling in-the-moment repairs down the line to avoid safety issues or delayed solutions.

Education & TrainingÂ

When it comes to manufacturing, real-time digital twins can help provide manufacturing students with the opportunity to practice their skills with real-time simulations from actual systems. Digital twins give students the opportunity for hands-on learning without the risk of causing real-life problems.

Training and educational videos can also benefit from real-time, people-type digital twins. Platforms like Tavus can generate thousands of videos without the labor and long wait times of traditional recording. Educators can also choose to implement AI video-based chatbots where a digital twin can interact with human users in real time, providing an engaging chat experience.

ManufacturingÂ

Digital twins in manufacturing are used to replicate processes or systems. Manufacturers use these replicas to analyze, monitor, design, or optimize their processes without having to work in the field or conduct expensive physical trials.

With real-time digital twins, manufacturers can extend the usefulness of these replicas to allow for continuous monitoring of plants or other digital manufacturing processes. They can identify areas for potential optimization, safety issues, and more and conduct the needed repairs or changes immediately, saving time and money and increasing efficiency.

Benefits of Real-Time Digital Twins

Letâs explore the benefits of real-time integration for digital twins.

Enhances PersonalizationÂ

A primary benefit of real-time digital twins is the development of conversational AI, which uses natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning to understand the context and intent of user inquiries. The ability to process human input and respond in a natural, conversational way enhances personalization and encourages connection between brands and their audiences.Â

Increases Accuracy & ReliabilityÂ

A traditional digital twin is still a static model if it does not implement real-time data and analysis, creating the possibility that it may be accurate at one point and then potentially obsolete the next. A constant stream of data allows real-time digital twins to evolve alongside their physical counterparts with precision.

Improves Predictive CapabilitiesÂ

The moment-by-moment updates provided by real-time digital twins allows for immediate feedback on problems and trends. Such swift insights allow for improved predictive capabilities and provide opportunities to fix problems before or as they happen.

Improved predictive capabilities also benefit people-type digital twins. With the ability to predict where a conversation will go or what types of responses are most common for certain types of human input, real-time digital twins can improve how natural their interactions are.

Saves Time & MoneyÂ Â

For traditional digital twins, even the best infrastructure needs time for data to travel from its source to processing units, causing delays. With real-time digital twins, that travel and processing time is cut down to milliseconds, saving organizations time. And faster speeds mean problems can be addressed sooner, saving money on delayed repairs and time for systems to be shut down.

Challenges of Real-Time Digital Twins

Implementing real-time capabilities in digital twins introduces some challenges. Transmission delays still exist, even with the best infrastructure, and complex or high-volume processing can also introduce low latency. Add in network congestion as a result of high traffic or limited bandwidth, and you may be left with even further delays.

Thatâs why itâs so important to choose the right technology for your real-time digital twin needs. For people-type digital twins, Tavus is one of the top choices on the market. Users need only invest the time of a single sample recording to create their digital twin, and then they can generate unlimited personalized videos in minutes!

How to Create a Real-Time Digital Twin for VideoÂ

Looking to create your own real-time digital twin for video with Tavus? Hereâs how to make AI videos in a nutshell.

Step 1: Record your general messageÂ

enriched_points.expanded_content: Digital twins serve as accurate digital representations of real-world infrastructure, enabling detailed visualization and simulation of physical assets for better understanding and management.
These systems continuously integrate real-time data from IoT sensors, ensuring that digital twins reflect the current state of physical objects, enhancing decision-making accuracy and responsiveness.
Digital twins facilitate real-time monitoring of infrastructure performance, allowing for predictive analytics and proactive adjustments to optimize operations and maintenance strategies.

By leveraging digital twins, organizations can revolutionize infrastructure management, improving efficiency, reducing downtime, and fostering sustainable growth through data-driven insights and automation.
